description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed from horizontally stacked, weathered wooden logs, with the ends of the logs protruding at the corners. The roof is a steeply pitched gable style, covered in what appears to be thatch or a similar natural material, giving it a traditional, earthy appearance. The front facade features a large, open doorway and a single rectangular window, both lacking glass or shutters, which suggests a simple, utilitarian design. The logs are grayish in tone, indicating age or exposure to the elements, while the roof has a muted, straw-like color. The cabin sits directly on the ground with no visible foundation or steps, and there is no porch or overhang extending from the main structure. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for rural or park settings within an urban context.
description: A rustic log cabin with a steep gable thatched roof, open doorway, and single window, made from weathered wooden logs.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the rectangular, thatched gable roof and the log construction along the sides.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open doorway and single window, with the gable roof and log ends clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear top-down view highlighting the roof and the back wall of logs, with no openings visible.
description_view_3: Back view showing the solid log wall and the gable roof, with no doors or windows present.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, covered in thatch or similar natural material, with moderate overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: one medium-sized rectangular window on the front facade, asymmetrically placed to the right of the doorway; no other windows visible
attribute_door_placement: one large open doorway centered on the front facade
